title: A Day In The Life Of A Cargo Ship Deck Officer | Life At Sea
body: I work in the shipping industry myself, not on a vessel but I take care and supply a fleet of more than 50 tankers operating worldwide. (sea charts, log books etc.) Really refreshing to see what a day on a vessel really looks like. Greetings from Germany
author: @Mel_97

title: A Day In The Life Of A Cargo Ship Deck Officer | Life At Sea
body:
Seems personality is a huge factor aboard such working ship. As an old dude, Bryan I believe you have tons more communicative power than many doctors I’ve had over the years. You instill calm, confidence in your abilities, skill sets, moreover a deep sense of self that transfers good vibes towards others.

You make people feel all is well in your world. I world until now I never realized. Thank You 🙏 Bakersfield, California
author: @OldRelic3758

title: A Day In The Life Of A Cargo Ship Deck Officer | Life At Sea
body: Quality is amazing as always. I can understand it take alot of efforts to make such amazing videos. Thank you man

title: A Day In The Life Of A Cargo Ship Deck Officer | Life At Sea

title: A Day In The Life Of A Cargo Ship Deck Officer | Life At Sea
body: Went out and look for 'the life on a containership' to find out how those guys live who are stuck in the Suez canal... So now I know! 👍
author: @PepijnSchoemakerWebinars

title: A Day In The Life Of A Cargo Ship Deck Officer | Life At Sea
body: I fish the Sandy Hook Bay, NJ.  I am ALWAYS amazed at the size of those ships.  You men and women make it look easy !!!!  THANK YOU all for you hard work in keeping America supplied.  You are the un-sung heroes of our great Nation !!!  JJP
author: @seabee7071

title: A Day In The Life Of A Cargo Ship Deck Officer | Life At Sea
body: 50 yrs ago I was a second officer with BP Tankers.  Somethings stay the same others change.  6 on 6 off in port. When the pilot was onboard we were all in uniform.  No computers and no display screens obviously; I had 4000 charts which as second officer I had to keep up to date.  We did have decca for some coastal parts of the world, otherwise compass brgs and radar range, a pencil line on the chart in HB or 2B, can't quite remember.  Thanks for the memories!
author: @rudderboost4188

title: A Day In The Life Of A Cargo Ship Deck Officer | Life At Sea
body: COFFEE COFFEE COFFEE THAT MACHINE GETS ONE HELL OF A WORK OUT.😄😄😄. "Hey capt we ran outta coffee "what?? ". "Thats it i quit im done get me some plane tickets"

title: 6AM COLLEGE DAY IN MY LIFE 🍂 (productive, studying for finals, fall semester at USC)
body: Life hack (I do this)… if someone’s alarm is going and it’s bothering me, I call them and it stops
author: @sheispraise

title: 6AM COLLEGE DAY IN MY LIFE 🍂 (productive, studying for finals, fall semester at USC)
body: OMG GURL I UNDERSTAND THE PAIN. I had a roommate that would set her alarm every 15 mins from 4am to 6am and she WOULD NEVER wake up. I had patience the first few days then it turned into me and my other roommates shaking her to wake up and then eventually i take her phone and silence it myself. And now i've moved out of that godforsaken room for 2nd sem 😅.
author: @phunsized2848

title: 6AM COLLEGE DAY IN MY LIFE 🍂 (productive, studying for finals, fall semester at USC)
body: As someone who gets up early and has late-night classes, college in the early mornings & late at night when it's dark is some of the BEST times... I just love walking around and it's so quiet & peaceful, it makes me love my campus even more lol
author: @kayleer

title: 6AM COLLEGE DAY IN MY LIFE 🍂 (productive, studying for finals, fall semester at USC)
body: I live with roommates too, I understand too well what you experienced with her, be brave ! Just have an adult talk with her of how this is disrespectful. And like other comments : you should’ve wake her up even tho you are not close with her !
author: @Emma_hnlt

title: 6AM COLLEGE DAY IN MY LIFE 🍂 (productive, studying for finals, fall semester at USC)
body: I prowlild be the roommate with the alarm because I am a DEEP sleeper..I literally sleep through all of my alarms! So I have to see an alarm wayyy earlier than I need to so I can wake up at the time I need to…yeah..😂
author: @ToriLYSM

title: 6AM COLLEGE DAY IN MY LIFE 🍂 (productive, studying for finals, fall semester at USC)
body: Taco. Makayla, I understand how you feel about roommates. I had to share a room this year at university and I just didn't like it.
author: @caylaalbertyn

title: 6AM COLLEGE DAY IN MY LIFE 🍂 (productive, studying for finals, fall semester at USC)
body: that girl gave good tips for waking up early, put on music and start your routine. my mouthwash is in before i sit on the toilet sometimes to kick things off! a heater in the bathroom if its cold will also really help keep you from running back into your warm bed! also, idc if im already awake, that alarm sound infuriates me. i wouldve gone in and said “please shut off your alarm its been going off and woke me up” AS LOUDLY AS POSSIBLE, standing over her. if you havent already, i highly recommend “the 5AM Club” by Robin Sharma, there are some helpful gems in there!
author: @teddymarian9416

title: 6AM COLLEGE DAY IN MY LIFE 🍂 (productive, studying for finals, fall semester at USC)
body: My mom has random alarms during the day and sometimes leaves them ringing so I just call her phone then cancel it and it stops the alarm without me having to say or do anything.

title: Touring Every Florida College So You Don't Have To | (UF UM UCF FAU ETC)
body: I go to FGCU and I absolutely LOVE the school. I wish he talked more about the water and nature because that is what the whole campus revolves around. And that waterfront is literally the best because you can rent out boats, canoes, kayaks, paddle boards, etc. as well as a beautiful resort style pool and aquatic center. The place truly looks like a resort and it blows me away every day that I get to study there.
author: @nadiafalcone9665

title: Touring Every Florida College So You Don't Have To | (UF UM UCF FAU ETC)
body: I graduated from UCF in 1990. Back then, the school was small with an undergrad enrollment around 25K. It has completely changed now. It's too big in my opinion.

title: Touring Every Florida College So You Don't Have To | (UF UM UCF FAU ETC)
body: As a high school senior, who is currently looking Florida colleges, I toured UF and wasn’t as impressed by it as a lot of people are. The campus wasn’t really my vibe and everything felt very serious. I actually much preferred USF and UCF campuses and environments so much so that I didn’t end up applying to UF, but it was really interesting to see your takes on the campuses!
author: @bubbleowl1539

author: @willasullivan8222

title: Touring Every Florida College So You Don't Have To | (UF UM UCF FAU ETC)
body: Love being an Owl alumni! It’s a completely different campus than when I was there, there was no engineering building, they hadn’t built the gym and new pool until my senior year, and we still had to drive to some random high school stadium 30 minutes away because the stadium hadn’t been built. The rapid development of that campus has been amazing to watch, and I love getting tickets that have access to the full service bar IN THE STADIUM! How awesome is that?!
author: @Alex-cz4fk

title: if USC were honest #usc #college #collegeadmissions #collegestudytips #ucla
body: You missed out on USC being in South Central LA, so the wealthy college kids are always getting their pockets run by locals. It seems like its been on a decline since the pandemic, but I remember that issue being real bad 10 ish years ago
author: @ablatnik

title: if USC were honest #usc #college #collegeadmissions #collegestudytips #ucla
body:
Hey!

I’m a graduate from the 90s!!

It was still a prestigious school, even back then.

I believe USC was ranked 51st in the nation out of 1,500 colleges when I graduated…

Just not in the top 25 or so like today.

Unless you use Forbes where I think USC was 14 last year?

They’ve changed those rankings and downgraded them unfortunately so the rankings are a little diluted.

But ranking isn’t everything… I had some of the best times of my life while there!
author: @sandramiller1988

title: university of south carolina | pros and cons
body: Hello Everyone! If you are just seeing this video and have any questions about USC, comment them here or dm them to me on instagram @dev_meets_world. I will be filming a q + a, tips on how I got into usc and more info I have from my personal experience! Ill be filming this in the next few days so ask all the questions you have and ill answer in the video or ill respond to you privately ❤️

author: @Lashaylovely_

title: university of south carolina | pros and cons
body: I stumbled across your channel with a different video and then saw this. I'm an alumni and this made me miss the campus so much. Seeing people play frisbee in the horseshoe and all the hidden gardens. Had so many fond memories and I also agree with most of the cons. I had a pretty good advisor all 4 years that supported me, so I was fortunate and my teachers were some of the best. Most of my close friends are from uni. The study abroad office helped me out a lot too when I decided to study kinda last minute in Korea for the summer and now I've been over here almost 7 years. I'm glad to see you have had some good times. Those trains though, I'll never miss those haha
author: @ItsAishaAbroad
